reasoning: The informal statement is the triangle inequality for a finite sum of complex numbers: |z_1 + ... + z_n| ≤ |z_1| + ... + |z_n|. The Lean formalization quantifies over n : ℕ and f : ℕ → ℂ, encoding the sequence z_i as f i for i ∈ range n. In Mathlib, ‖z‖ for z : ℂ is exactly Complex.abs z = |z|, so the norm matches the informal absolute value. The quantification over f : ℕ → ℂ is the standard Lean idiom for "arbitrary finite sequence of length n"; since only values f 0, ..., f (n-1) are used, this is logically equivalent to quantifying over a finite sequence of length n. The n = 0 case (empty sum, 0 ≤ 0) is trivially true and does not make the statement stronger or weaker than the informal intent. The formalization is faithful.
